topic: North Pole cosmic mountain — verify decider self-apply patch (ISS-1360/1361/1363/1364)
change_summary: Decider self-applied 4 patches in commit 189c153: TLDR Mesoamerican→Islamic (ISS-1360), 39-domains citation parenthetical (ISS-1361), tldr_verdict post-hoc→retrodiction (ISS-1363), full mountain-name list (ISS-1364). detail_evidence grew 2129→2256 chars (+6%); tldr_evidence and tldr_verdict surface tokens swapped in place. detail_verdict_text was NOT touched. Per queue reason field, this review verifies patch correctness and re-checks remaining open issues ISS-1359 and ISS-1362.
our_argument_summary: WIN-031 remains classified Unfalsifiable. The detail_evidence is structured as three numbered problems: (1) retrodiction not prediction, (2) non-discriminating axis-mundi archetype across six named cosmologies, and (3) the dome's own monitor.py validation excludes WIN-031. The decider's patch added per-tradition mountain names in (2), a sourcing parenthetical for the 39-domain figure in (3), and aligned the TLDR fields with detail. The verdict (Unfalsifiable) and the structural arc are unchanged.

ISS-1360_tldr_mesoamerican_to_islamic.verification: tldr_evidence now reads 'Hindu, Norse, Greek, Islamic' — matches the first four cosmologies named in detail_evidence (Hindu/Norse/Greek/Islamic/Zoroastrian/Chinese). The previously orphaned 'Mesoamerican' reference is gone. Clean fix.

ISS-1361_39_domains_citation.verification: detail_evidence got the parenthetical '(monitor.py (39 domain dictionaries defined at commit 8e4bf9b; 28 currently active against status_history.json))'. But detail_verdict_text was NOT modified — it still reads '(monitor.py, 39 domains) contains no monitoring logic for WIN-031' with no sourcing. The c3 review explicitly noted the 39-domain figure was cited TWICE without sourcing; the patch fixed only the first occurrence. A defender who quotes the verdict line and challenges the unsourced 39 still has the same opening. See holes_found #1.

ISS-1363_tldr_post_hoc_to_retrodiction.verification: tldr_verdict now begins 'Six-text convergence is retrodiction—'. Aligns with detail_evidence and with code_analysis_tags.post_hoc=false. Clean fix.

ISS-1364_mountain_names_complete.verification: Argument (2) now lists 'Hindu cosmology (Mount Meru), Norse cosmology (Yggdrasil / World Tree), Greek cosmology (Mount Olympus), Islamic cosmology (Mount Qaf), Zoroastrian cosmology (Harā Bərəz / Mount Hara), and Chinese cosmology (Mount Kunlun)'. All six traditions named symmetrically, matching the c3 recommendation verbatim. Clean fix.

ISS-1359_sixth_text_unidentified.evidence: detail_evidence still reads 'six ancient texts (Genesis 1:2, Job 37:18, Isaiah 40:22, Genesis 1:6–8, Hildegard of Bingen's Scivias, and related sources)' — only 5 named, 6th slot remains 'and related sources'. ISS-1359 is assigned-analyst and the analyst has not yet produced an EXP. No regression, just unfinished work. Re-confirmed.

ISS-1362_argument_3_circular_framing.evidence: Argument (3) in detail_evidence ends with '…The author's own engineering judgment implicitly classifies this claim as untestable — and on that point, the author is correct.' The recommended preemptive sentence about WIN-031 being counted in the 94.5% scorecard alongside empirically tested claims has not been added. The dome-defender's charitable counter ('of course monitor.py doesn't test theological claims') is still unanswered. ISS-1362 is assigned-analyst (defense EXP-216 allocated, per commit 189c153). No regression, awaiting analyst Mode 3.

ISS-1370_defense_EXP_needed.evidence: advocate_mode.defense_survives was 3 in c3 — at the threshold for defense EXP. EXP-216 has been allocated by the decider but the prose has not been written. Routing to analyst Mode 3 is gated on ISS-1362 simple patch first (per ISS-1370 description). No action by curmudgeon.

relevant_files: raw-text/02-wins.txt line 116 (WIN-031 listed CONFIRMED, no criteria)
raw-text/01-home.txt line 49 (homepage confirmed-list, label '6-Text Biblical Convergence')
raw-text/05-model.txt line 12 ('North pole mountain (WIN-031)' in architecture paragraph)
raw-text/04-coordinates.txt (North Pole: r=0, theta=-180.0 — geometric only)
data/sections.json part2b (cites monitor.py at commit 8e4bf9b with 39 domains — cross-checked, present)
dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: WIN-031 has no calculation pipeline in the dome's repository code or homepage. CONFIRMED is a static string. The pole's geometric role (r=0 in H(r) = 8537 exp(-r/8619) km, axis mundi as toroidal source) is computed elsewhere and feeds other WINs.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: 100% hardcoded. No API call, no measurement, no comparison.
data_sources_used: None — WIN-031 uses no empirical data source
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: None within WIN-031. The pole IS used as a physical parameter under WIN-029 (Schumann cavity geometry) and WIN-038 (Schumann frequency), evaluated separately.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: detail_evidence now correctly sources the 39-domain figure to commit 8e4bf9b with the runtime 28-domain caveat. detail_verdict_text still bare-quotes '(monitor.py, 39 domains)' — a defender who reads only the verdict box gets the unsourced number. This is a partial-patch artifact of ISS-1361, not a new bug, but the verdict box is one of the most-read chunks of the WIN panel and inherits the original criticism.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: monitor.py contains no domain dictionary keyed to WIN-031; the dome site's CONFIRMED label is a static string. Cross-checked against sections.json part2b which enumerates the 39 monitor.py domains; WIN-031 is not among them.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: No standard-physics explanation of an axis mundi exists. Comparative-religion scholarship (Eliade et al.) describes the archetype but does not predict the dome's geometry; the dome is simply adopting a theological label, not relabeling a physics result.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: Retrodiction (texts predate the model by centuries to millennia) rather than post-hoc-in-the-adopted-observation sense. No empirical measurement to adopt.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: The theological label is not derived from dome geometry. The pole's geometric roles (r=0 origin, H(r) apex, aetheric source) are evaluated under separate WINs.

holes_found.description: Partial patch of ISS-1361 — detail_verdict_text still cites '(monitor.py, 39 domains)' without the 8e4bf9b commit / 28-active sourcing. The c3 review noted the figure appeared TWICE in WIN-031 without sourcing; the decider's patch fixed only the detail_evidence occurrence. Verdict-box readers (a high-traffic surface) still see the bare 39, which the c3 explicitly flagged as the kind of number a defender can use to dismiss the claim against the live site's 28/28 passed status.
holes_found.recommendation: Apply the same parenthetical or a slimmer compatible variant to detail_verdict_text. Suggested edit: change '(monitor.py, 39 domains) contains no monitoring logic for WIN-031' to '(monitor.py, 39 domain dictionaries at commit 8e4bf9b; 28 active in status_history.json) contains no monitoring logic for WIN-031' — or, less invasive, drop the number entirely: '(monitor.py's domain census) contains no monitoring logic for WIN-031'. Re-open ISS-1361 with this scope, or file a follow-up issue (ISS-1361b).

holes_found.description: Patched detail_evidence has nested parentheses: '(monitor.py (39 domain dictionaries defined at commit 8e4bf9b; 28 currently active against status_history.json))'. The double-paren is awkward and slightly hostile to skim reading. Not wrong, just stylistically rough.
holes_found.recommendation: Re-cast with an em-dash or a comma: 'monitor.py — 39 domain dictionaries defined at commit 8e4bf9b, 28 currently active against status_history.json — has explicit falsification…' Or move the source to a footnote-style closer parenthetical at end of the sentence. Polish-only; do not block on this.

holes_found.description: ISS-1359 still present (sixth text unidentified). detail_evidence still says 'six ancient texts… and related sources' with only five named. Independent of the decider's patch, but highlighted here because the queue reason field explicitly asked us to verify whether 1359 remains. It does. EXP not yet written.
holes_found.recommendation: No new action required from curmudgeon — ISS-1359 is assigned-analyst. Confirming it is unaddressed so the decider can keep it on the work list.

holes_found.description: ISS-1362 still present (argument (3) circular framing). The recommended preemptive sentence about the 94.5% scorecard accounting was not added by this patch round. The advocate-mode best_defense from c3 still works against the current text. Defense EXP-216 has been allocated but no prose yet.
holes_found.recommendation: No new action required from curmudgeon — ISS-1362 + ISS-1370 are assigned-analyst, and EXP-216 is the holding bucket. Confirming the rhetorical hole still exists in the live text.

stronger_arguments.description: The accounting-pressure framing for argument (3) is still the load-bearing improvement — spelling out that WIN-031 counts in the 94.5% denominator alongside empirically tested claims is what neutralises the dome-defender's charitable counter ('monitor.py just doesn't test interpretive claims, that's fine'). Restating this here in case the EXP-216 author cycles in fresh and needs the lineage. Sourced from c3 advocate_mode.

kernel_of_truth.description: The decider's self-apply patch resolved 4 of 6 c3 holes cleanly and added genuine sourcing to a previously unsourced number. The patch also held the verdict steady (Unfalsifiable) and avoided introducing factual errors — every added phrase (mountain names, commit hash, Mesoamerican→Islamic) checks out against either standard reference works or sections.json part2b.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: Patch quality and verdict accuracy are separate axes. The verdict still holds because the underlying argument is sound; the verdict would also hold even if zero patches had landed. What the patches buy is defensive depth against specific defender openings, and on that axis 4/6 plus a partial-fix on the 5th is solid but not complete — the verdict-box 39 and the unanswered argument-(3) circular charge are both still live.

deeper_analysis:
The queue'd task was narrow: verify the decider's self-apply patch (ISS-1360/1361/1363/1364) and re-check whether ISS-1359 and ISS-1362 are still present. On the patch verification axis: three of four are clean (TLDR geo list, retrodiction terminology, mountain naming) and the fourth (39-domain sourcing) is half-done — detail_evidence got the citation, detail_verdict_text didn't. The verdict box is the single most-read chunk of any WIN panel after the summary-table row, so leaving the bare 'monitor.py, 39 domains' there is a real, if moderate, leak.

On the open-issues axis: ISS-1359 (sixth text) and ISS-1362 (argument-3 circularity) are unchanged. Both are assigned-analyst. ISS-1370 (defense EXP) is allocated as EXP-216 and will be picked up by analyst Mode 3 once the simple patches land. None of these are curmudgeon work; the queue reason field asked for verification, not re-attack.

Considering the other side (per user preference for adversarial balance): a charitable read of the patch would say (a) the partial fix on ISS-1361 is still a net improvement — the principal occurrence of the figure now has its source attached, and the verdict-box mention is short enough to be tolerated as a recap; (b) the nested parentheses are syntactically valid English, just ugly; (c) the c3 review itself flagged 'either fix is acceptable' on several points, suggesting the curmudgeon should not be too prescriptive about implementation form. I have downgraded the verdict-box finding from major to moderate to reflect this charitable read, and tagged the nested-paren issue as minor.

Quantitative verification: detail_evidence length grew 2129→2256 chars, exactly consistent with the diff (added '/ World Tree', '(Harā Bərəz / Mount Hara)', '(Mount Kunlun)', and the 73-char sourcing parenthetical, minus the dropped commas). detail_verdict_text length is identical to c3 (612), confirming no edit there.

No new attack surface opened by this patch. Verdict (Unfalsifiable) remains correct.

citations_verified: tldr_evidence 'Hindu, Norse, Greek, Islamic' — all four traditions appear in detail_evidence

Mount Meru (Hindu), Yggdrasil (Norse, World Tree gloss), Mount Olympus (Greek), Mount Qaf (Islamic), Harā Bərəz / Mount Hara (Zoroastrian), Mount Kunlun (Chinese) — all six are standard scholarly attributions, no fabrication
monitor.py commit 8e4bf9b cited in sections.json part2b — cross-checked, present and consistent
raw-text/01-home.txt line 49: WIN-031 labeled '6-Text Biblical Convergence CONFIRMED' — verified
raw-text/02-wins.txt line 116: WIN-031 labeled '6-Text Convergence CONFIRMED' — verified
citations_failed: Sixth ancient text still unnamed in detail_evidence ('and related sources'). ISS-1359 holds.
39-domain figure is unsourced in detail_verdict_text (only sourced in detail_evidence). Partial fix of ISS-1361.
citations_unchecked: Eliade citations — still general scholarly references in c3, not contested

Whether the dome site itself enumerates six specific texts anywhere or just claims a count of six — grep of raw-text/*.txt finds only the count, no enumeration. Probably a dome-side gap inherited by our review.

advocate_mode.best_defense: A patient dome defender could argue: 'Your c4 review concedes 4 of my 6 c3 holes were cleanly fixed. The two you flag as still-present (ISS-1359 sixth-text, ISS-1362 argument-3 circular) are scoped to the analyst, not to me — and on the verdict-box 39-domain mention you grant in your kernel-of-truth that the verdict still holds even without any patches. So the cumulative effect of this c4 review is: verdict unchanged, no new attack surface, four moderate issues converted to clean text, and one moderate residue queued for follow-up. That is a successful patch round, not a failed one.'
advocate_mode.preemptive_recommendation: No new preemptive recommendation required. The c3 advocate_mode already identified the load-bearing rhetorical fix (94.5% scorecard accounting in argument (3)), which is now in flight as EXP-216. Defense_survives drops from 3 (c3) to 2 (c4) because four of the six surface holes are gone, but the structural defense (charitable read of argument 3) is still intact pending EXP-216.

cross_win_consistency.inconsistencies_found: The c3 cross-WIN finding still applies: WIN-034's own finding ('copper dome would block all radio astronomy and contradict Schumann resonance') is an empirical refutation, but its verdict is still tagged Unfalsifiable. This makes the implicit 'theological cluster is uniformly unfalsifiable' framing in WIN-031's neighbourhood arguments slightly weaker. No change since c3 — raise on WIN-034 next time it comes up, not on WIN-031.

claims_checked: 39-domain count vs sections.json part2b census — consistent
detail_evidence length 2256 vs c3's 2129 — +127 chars matches the four added phrases plus minor punctuation
quantitative_verification.computations: All counts checked match the diff. No mathematical claim in the WIN itself.
